name: Middle Korean (10th-16th cent.)

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Middle Korean.

notes: Historical stage of Koreanic (10th-16th century), written in early Hangul after 1443 with many now-obsolete jamo (arae-a, yesieung, pansios and other letters, plus obsolete consonant clusters) and marked with a pitch-accent tone-dot system. The text is encoded as precomposed syllable blocks, and recovering grapheme-to-IPA requires jamo decomposition together with a per-word philological pronunciation stratum for the obsolete letters that the engine does not bundle; engine coverage is therefore lexicon-dependent and effectively zero without that stratum. Left empty deliberately rather than faking single-codepoint syllable rules; the tone-dot marks are not encoded.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Middle Korean
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
